designFaqs.q1: What does the design consultation include?
designFaqs.a1: Our design consultation includes a discussion of your vision, space assessment, material recommendations, 3D visualization when applicable, and a detailed project plan with timeline and budget estimate.
designFaqs.q2: Do you offer 3D renderings of renovation designs?
designFaqs.a2: Yes, we provide 3D visualizations for many projects so you can see exactly how your renovated space will look before any work begins.
designFaqs.q3: How much does a design consultation cost?
designFaqs.a3: Our initial design consultations are free. We believe in helping you explore your options before making any commitment.
designFaqs.q4: How long does the design process take before construction begins?
designFaqs.a4: The design phase typically takes 1–3 weeks depending on project complexity. Simple single-room designs move faster; multi-room or whole-house designs include additional revision rounds. Once you approve the design, we move directly into scheduling and procurement.
designFaqs.q5: What happens after I approve the design?
designFaqs.a5: After design approval, we finalize material selections, apply for any required permits, and create a detailed construction schedule. Your designer stays involved through construction to ensure the build matches the approved vision.
designFaqs.q6: Can I see design renderings for multiple rooms at once?
designFaqs.a6: Yes. For whole-house and multi-room renovations, we create coordinated renderings for all spaces so you can review material palettes, finishes, and furniture layouts as a cohesive design before committing to any single room.
